title: ProductPricingTermAndConditionTypeValues
description: In banking, Product Pricing Terms and Conditions (T&Cs) can be classified into several types, depending on the product and how pricing is structured.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Interest Rate Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description: Defines how interest/profit is calculated and applied (fixed, floating, tiered, risk-based, linked to benchmark rates).

x-enumeration-literals.name: Fees & Charges Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description: Covers all non-interest costs such as processing fees, annual/renewal fees, transaction charges, penalties, and prepayment/early settlement fees.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Tenure & Repayment Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description: Specifies the product duration (tenure/maturity) and repayment or payout structure (installments, bullet payment, frequency, grace periods).

x-enumeration-literals.name: Collateral & Security Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description: Conditions related to security backing the product (secured vs. unsecured, guarantees, liens, pledges, hypothecation).

x-enumeration-literals.name: Usage & Operational Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description: Rules governing how the product can be used (withdrawal/credit limits, currency restrictions, product-specific conditions, minimum balances).

x-enumeration-literals.name: Regulatory & Compliance Term and Condition
x-enumeration-literals.description: Mandatory legal/regulatory requirements (disclosure of APR/effective rate, KYC/AML checks, central bank pricing caps/floors, deposit insurance).
